source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Anna uses 32 small grey squares to frame a $7 \mathrm{~cm}$ by $7 \mathrm{~cm}$ big picture. How many small grey squares does she have to use to frame a $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ by $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ big picture?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question text is self-contained and does not explicitly reference a picture, diagram, or visual feature
An image asset exists in the registry, but the wording appears answerable from text alone
multi_step: The prompt gives one framing example (7 cm by 7 cm uses 32 squares) and asks for a different size (10 cm by 10 cm)
This requires inferring a relationship for the border/frame from the first case and then applying it to the second case
The task is more than direct lookup but remains a short mathematical reasoning chain
verifiability: answer_text is a single exact value: 44
The result can be checked by explicit border-count arithmetic derived from the prompt

decision_reasons: Asset registry is complete: question, answer, and image are all present, with no recorded issues and registry_passed=true
Core semantics are recoverable from the text alone, so the sample remains usable even though an image asset is present
Verifiability is very high because the answer is a single exact numeric value
The problem requires some reasoning beyond direct lookup, giving it useful task value
The only risk flag is that the image may not be explicitly needed, which does not undermine sample usability
